C[arnfasta: Look at L16, was considering K18, L18 as well.  K18 seemed to do the same thing as L18  but also put more pressure on white.  L18 was more solid but K18 didn't seem to be too risky.  Then I became concerned that white would get too strong a presence at the top center if I only played toward the edge so I thought I'd go L16. L16 lets me strengthen the L17 stone, create more support for the O16 group and give me a better foothold toward the center

C[arnfasta: I debated L15 and M15 before going with M14.  Similar to the last move, I thought M15 accomplished the same general purpose of L15 but also more directly strengthened the O16 group.  My concern with L15 is that it didnt' support the O16 group enough and only encouraged white to push toward the center.  The M15 I felt like didn't extend far enough.  So I decided on M14 because, from my reading, the pokes didn't look deadly and it would get me out farther while still lending support to the O16 group

C[arnfasta: I was thinking of ignoring R17 and playing elsewhere but then I was worried that  a followup play at O18 would be powerful.  O18 would threaten L18 which would leave me without any eye space on the top and O18 could be used with Q18 to setup a ladder (I think)
C[arnfasta: With K18, I thought about playing elsewhere first but I wanted an easy resolve before moving on.

C[arnfasta: with F15, I felt like I could ignore the F17 stone.  I wanted to secure a foothold on the upper left and felt like I still had space to do it but if I didn't act soon, I'd get smothered by the K18 group making it hard to gain enough space.  By jumping to F15, I can maintain an equal pace down the board and open up opportunities to undermine the left side
C[arnfasta: E13 was my attempt to do a pressure the left side while still extending and getting some breathing room.  I'm also looking at the bottom left still.
C[arnfasta: O3 is play I'd like to do, secure the bottom right.  But trying to resolve the upper left first.

arnfasta: I'm thinking of first getting out into the center but also, possibly, using my group on the right side to squeeze the center top white group.  It's still killable but that isn't my first priority.  Securing points and denying territory is.  With the right center position I have (Q11), I feel like I'm in a decent position.
C[arnfasta: I struggled a lot with move 78. I thought about G7 and G5 but they both seemed to make me more vulnerable and not accomplsih what I wanted: which was to capture white's C8 group
column2: then I thought I might be missing the point and started exploring H5 to try and open up possibilities and options for myself

column1: arnfasta: During that, I noticed F7 and thought it was more important to go for F7 now and then return to H5, which still seems like a good move, later
arnfasta: B H5, W G5, B G4 to try and threaten bottom right and bottom center
C[arnfasta: Anticipating either E10 or C9 as a response.  With C9, I'll B10, let white take D9 if they want.  I'm not worried about 1 eye, just 2.

arnfasta: With E10, I was thinking of going C9 but I think that'll get me killed because I won't have a reliable way out of white response to C9 with C12.  So instead I'm looking at B10 to give the C10 a little extra strength and give myself ways to push into the upper left and lower left.

arnfasta: I still need to read through it more.  Worst case, I'm planning to go B11 or C12 and push into the top left. Either way, I'd like to threaten the upper left with this group to follow up with pressure in the top left F17 group.  I'd like to use a D18 play to get some leverage and followup with a G18 play to crush down on that top middle white group.  But I suspect I'll need to K10/K11 first to prevent a false ladder.  if I can secure that and shut down the danger of the cut at H9, it'll also help me pressure the top center group and creature more structure for my center group.  So that's top priority as soon as i can resolve the left side to a place where I feel like there's no immediate threats I need to be worried about

arnfasta: I think that point is if it goes: White E10, Black B10, White C12 (may be overestimating its importance although I think a black C13 to C12 gives me nice leverage) would let me then play K11.  at that point (white C12), if white tries to take the D12 group with E12, Black takes at E11, white plays F11, black closes E12, white connects with F10 and then black plays G11 to be fine.  If white then turns to attack the C10 group with C9, black can C13 to threaten breaking into the upper left.  If white responds, black follows up with B11 to take the group.

arnfasta: I also still think I have opportunities in the bottom left to use that M2 group to threaten.  And at the right side, looking at T9 and R4/S4 to see if I can set something up to whittle that down to one eye.  Also, O4 looks nice for a poke but P3 shuts down the possibility to threaten the N3 group

arnfasta: but if I can somehow get O4 and P3 then I can take those stones.  I'm trying to figure out if there's another way for me to force that kind of play.  Likely a play with the R3 group.  I might also want to let white connect with O4 and push out to O6 in order to lure them into a false sense of security but I think there'd be too many liberties to make that work

C[arnfasta: So with F10, I'm thinking that white goes for C9, I'll play C12, white takes at E10, I'll respond with F9, white will play F10, I'll close with D10.  White then goes F11 and I'll respond with G11.  Because I've got C12, I'm not worried about the surround, I've got a nice wall toward the center and a small opening with C12 into the upper right.

arnfasta: I suspect that white will then close C10. I could still try to kill but I'd rather play bigger first and return later. I still am not good at gauging points when things are so unsettled but I think I'm still safe
arnfasta: so I'd rather shore up the K11 space (the false ladder) or pressure the top left (or lower left) before squeezing the center left
arnfasta: if white decides to immediately make eyes, I think I can take advantage of that to make bigger strides

C[arnfasta: So with C13, I'm trying to go B - C13, W - D14, B - B11, W - B12, B - B9, W - A11, B - C9 in order to lockdown that C8 group.  or really ""lockdown,"" I'm still concerned with C6 and E6 but I don't think I need to act first there though I'll read it again.  I'll lose initiative but I think it's worth it to keep things separated.  I think I can keep thinsg under control in the center if white cuts with H9 by playing at K9 or K10.  Need to read out both to be sure.

arnfasta: also, in theory, I can use the C13 stone (if things go down as I expect then I'll need to A9 first) but the C13 stone can be used to threaten capturing the C12 white group with a play by black at B13.

arnfasta: Even if white decides to C9, which is the only other move I'm seeing atm, at least in the area, then I'll either defend and continue the sequence slightly delayed or take D14 and make a trade for the upper right.  Not sure which yet if that's the route. But the threat at D13 seems a little too big to ignore

C[arnfasta: Decided I should try to squeeze the top right first with B16 and D18 (with a black followup  of G18 to make it harder to make two eyes for the white J18 group). Still got an eye toward K11
arnfasta: Made the one point jump to B16 because I figured even if white pushes to B15, A15 can use the corner to keep me safe

C[arnfasta: I misread this and though that W:A18, B:A16 would result in white going B18 but, of course, white can go B19 to accomplish the same thing ANd get an eye.  I think I can still capture with D18 but we'll see

C[arnfasta: The path I'm seeing right now is: W: A18, B:A16, W:B19, B:D18, W:C18 (to protect the second eye), B:C16, W:D17 (to put pressure on the D18 stone and prevent the D17 cut if W goes D16), B:D16 and takes the group.  White gets the upper right corner but with F19, I'm not worried about white moving along the edge
arnfasta: apparently i was wrong, white goes for C16

C[arnfasta: The thought here is that it's okay if white goes B16, I'll go A14, white probably goes A18 to prevent a ko and then I'll take B19 to prevent an easy second eye. D18 helps strengthen the B19 play that comes later.
C[arnfasta: I was misreading and not seeing the simple B18 play white can do to make A17 a second eye.  I can still deny extra points with B19 though.
arnfasta: I think though that the B19 should be used later.  I'll go for K11 next.  The B19 could be a nice forcing play but doesn't gain me much and doesn't take away a lot so it can be used later

C[arnfasta: Thought about going L13 here instead but was worried about L14 as a response. B goes L12 to keep the foothold and then white plays M15 and cuts into the L16 group. SO I thought L14 both strengthens the M14 stone and threatens to cut in 2 places

C[arnfasta: Little confused here. Not sure whether to go M15 or L13. I like L13 because it divides the K13 and M13 stones and while I might lose a few points up top, that group still seems stable.  On the otherhand, M15 still keeps the possibility of capture open and keeps things together a little more strongly.  Going with M15 because while white will likely go L13, I can still go J14 and pressure with the two cutting points

C[arnfasta: I thought about going L12 but while it would offer two cutting points, I figured I could push first with J14 and then still followup with L13 later if I wanted.  So I want to create more cutting points first and then try to exploit the weakness of having so many

C[arnfasta: Trying to decide between S11 or P11 but I'm going S11 because it keeps open teh possibility of forcing the top right (not sure it's actually a possibility but looks like it might be doable)
C[arnfasta: I don't know if this will work but I""m looking at: B:J12, W:J13, B:H13, W:H14, B:L13, W:M12, B:L10, W:M11, B:M9, W:N9, B:L9
arnfasta: and then I have two cutting points to play with.  And the extension with S10 should give me enough liberties if I can make enough forcing plays

C[arnfasta: Trying to capture that right side group of white stones.  B:P16 and O15 to shore up the black center group.  I don't think this'll trigger white because if it looks like I'm trying to take the P15 group, white sees that a Q15 play will negate that.  If this is what white sees and doesn't feel threatened then on black's next turn B:S9, W:S8, B:T14, W:Q15, B:T15, W:T16, B:T13. If white goes T9 to lock it the group down then I return with T18.  If white goes S18 then I go T17 and if white goes T18 then I think I'm behind by 1 liberty.  I'll reread if I can get P16 and O15 in and the S13 group hasnt' been locked down

arnfasta: Currently at move 183: still need to test B19. The B17 white group is alive with a B18 or C18 play followed by a B18.  But I don't lose anything if i play it right and I can cut potential points.  The B10 group is alive, I can get shave a point or two with A7.  Still opportunities to push with K4 and reduce territory some more.  Same with Q3
column2: was looking at P2 but the result is similar.  So small reduction opportunity there.  S3 might also be a way to snag a tiny corner. In the upper center, I'm watching G18 because I need a followup at H18 to shutdown potential eyes.  If I can do that then there's only 1 eye and the group is dead.  F10/G11 to help secure my left center black group.  Not concerned with the M8 group, those stones appear dead.

C[arnfasta: I think I'm still safe here. The big vulnerability is F5 followed by E6.  If white goes F5, black can go F4.  If white ignores and pushes through with F6, black responds with F3 to force a response at F2.  Black then follows up with G4 and either goes G5 or H4, whichever white doesn't choose.  Now black has leverage on the bottom right and a way out.

arnfasta: If white goes F3...B:F6, W:G5 and black is connected.  If W:G5, B:F6,W:E6 then I'll be looking at H3 as a possible way to make capture or at least reduce.  The center black group isn't settled but still has plenty of room to maneuver.  And the bottom left should be able to make two eyes.
C[arnfasta: ah shoot. I wanted to go K13 instead of J13 because J13 looks too loose and I think white can punch through but I'll have to adjust now

C[arnfasta: thought about K12 but it opens up 2 cutting points and I can't read out a way to keep myself from getting into a lot trouble so I'm strengthening one group first and then looking to push in
C[arnfasta: I was not expecting B5 but I can threaten B6 with my C6 play and then use D7 to take the group. OR I can push in with B6 and start to whittle down liberties for the larger group as well
arnfasta: so overall I think it's a win-win for me

Kuroneko: the idea is that, in a correspondence game
Kuroneko: there's a lot of ""obvious"" moves that are played
Kuroneko: and conditional moves allow you to set up a sequence in advance
Kuroneko: so you can set a string of, for example: black B-7, white A-9, black B-2, white D-15
arnfasta: that makes so much sense
Kuroneko: if you're playing white, and black plays B-7, the server will automatically play A-9 for you
Kuroneko: same with black B-2 and white D-15
Kuroneko: so you can save a ton of time in places like the endgame
C[Kuroneko: i believe it originated from correspondence chess servers
Kuroneko: and go servers like OGS started adopting it
Kuroneko: the problem is that, to set up conditional moves, you can also play out sequences on a board
Kuroneko: e.g. playing out a difficult sequence
Kuroneko: so if you disable game analysis (which is what that's meant for), disabling conditional moves is a natural extension of that

arnfasta: when possible, look to be as spread out as you can, you don't want to be so loose that you're super easy to invade. But so many stones in one corner, in the early game, gives me an advantage
arnfasta: because I'm more spread out
arnfasta: with the group in the middle, the N7 group, try to read out if and when you need ot take the group. It was a complicated setup so it's not a bad idea to play it safer
arnfasta: BUT once they were effectively dead, I was able to get a couple more points while you invested moves in stones that were dead
arnfasta: it IS really good to keep reevaluating and looking at the whole board
arnfasta: but since we each have the same number of moves, it's important to think about what's the most effective. Though obviously, with more experience, that gets easier to evaluate
arnfasta: one thing I can see first off is that you left the whole right side of the board untouched for a longtime
arnfasta: you don't need to go crazy on the right side but by getting stones down there early, it sets you up to be able to use them later
arnfasta: but since there weren't any, I was free to develop a good portion of the upper right
arnfasta: Mov e155 with the F19, it's not a bad play but it denies me 1 point. It's a really small move. And you're not vulnerable if I play first
arnfasta: You would lose 1 point, IF you wanted to respond
arnfasta: so instead, I'd look for bigger moves like Q13, still smallish but bigger than one point
arnfasta: R18, which would also pressure the top right group and help give you a few more points
arnfasta: E10 to let you try to puncture my left group
arnfasta: K7 was a nice play, giving your center black group some room and pressuring down on my white group
arnfasta: I don't think move 81, L9 was really necessary
arnfasta: If I take L9 as white, you can push with L8 and it's gonna be really tough
arnfasta: plus you're still connected with K9 or L8
arnfasta: J4 instead for move 81 would have been stronger
arnfasta: it's creating territory for you on the bottom center and it's threatening my group more strongly
arnfasta: L9 is much more passive
arnfasta: which isn't always bad, soemtimes necessary, but I think you can get away here with really continuing the offenseive
arnfasta: the whole line gets much more complicated than I can read
arnfasta: if you stick with going for the kill
arnfasta: but I think you stand a better chance there
arnfasta: But back to bigger ideas and setups, don't be afraid to look for the more aggressive option like in Move 71
arnfasta: by just plugging the hole with J11, I get the ability to make a super solid wall
arnfasta: IF instead you press the attack with K12, the game gets much more interesting. You're not in the clear but you have a lot of avenues of attack
arnfasta: like I just connect with K14 and then you push out with L12
arnfasta: I know the center worked out but I'm not sure about move 83 with the extension. It feels a little slow and sluggish
arnfasta: I think a 1 point jump might have been better, so going to N9
arnfasta: It does the same thing as M9 but it gives you more distance and more opportunities to attack
arnfasta: Maybe the other big thing is just look for ways to pressure kills/captures to further other goals
arnfasta: it's great when you can get a kill but in this game, I tried to use my dead group to strengthen other groups by creating forcing moves, moves you have to answer
arnfasta: that's also why, if you think one of your groups is dead, see about playing elsewhere and coming back to it. Sometimes you can use that group later in a more developed game
arnfasta: Sorry XD That's a crazy mess of words.  Any specific questions? I'm still learning so my advice should all be taken with a grain of salt.
arnfasta: My teacher, Kuroneko on here, is a great resource though for questions. He used to stream but he still has some youtube stuff up KNMeepsie that goes over concepts much better than I can XD
